perturbation.original_text: All shipments of Product(s) shall be delivered [***] (lncoterms 2010) Sutro Facility. Title and risk of loss and damage to the Product(s) shall remain with Sutro until the Product(s) are delivered in accordance with the foregoing, at which time title and risk ofloss and damage to the Product(s) shall pass to SutroVax. SutroVax will arrange for shipping from Sutro's Facility to the destination specified by SutroVax at SutroVax's cost and expense.
perturbation.changed_text: All shipments of Product(s) shall be delivered [***] (lncoterms 2010) Sutro Facility. Title and risk of loss and damage to the Product(s) shall pass to SutroVax upon shipment from Sutro's facility. SutroVax will arrange for shipping from Sutro's Facility to the destination specified by SutroVax at SutroVax's cost and expense.
perturbation.explanation: This modification shifts the risk of loss to the buyer immediately upon shipment, which may violate California Commercial Code provisions that imply a warranty that goods are fit for purpose. The original clause adheres to Incoterms, whereas the modified version removes buyer protections afforded by those terms.
perturbation.law_explanation: California Commercial Code § 2315 implies a warranty that goods are fit for purpose, and shifting risk of loss to the buyer upon shipment contradicts that. Sutro could ship damaged or unusable products, and SutroVax would assume the risk of that loss. By assuming the risk of loss upon shipment, SutroVax is accepting responsibility for the state of the goods at the point of departure, regardless of whether the goods conform to required standards at that moment, which is in direct contradiction to the implied warranty of fitness for a particular purpose.
perturbation.scraped_snippet_1: California Code, COM 2315 skip to content home accessibility FAQ feedback sitemap login x Quick Search: Bill Number Bill Keyword Home Bill Information California Law Publications Other Resources My Subscriptions My Favorites California Law >> Code Search >> Code Section Code Section Code: Select Code CONS BPC CIV CCP COM CORP EDC ELEC EVID FAM FIN FGC FAC GOV HNC HSC INS LAB MVC PEN PROB PCC PRC PUC RTC SHC UIC VEH WAT WIC Article: Section: Code: Select Code All BPC CIV CCP COM CORP EDC ELEC EVID FAM FIN FGC FAC GOV HNC HSC INS LAB MVC PEN PROB PCC PRC PUC RTC SHC UIC VEH WAT WIC Section: Keyword(s): Code Search Text Search Up^ << Previous Next >> cross-reference chaptered bills PDF | Add To My Favorites Search Phrase: Code Text Commercial Code - COM DIVISION 2. SALES [2101 - 2801] ( Division 2 enacted by Stats. 1963, Ch. 819. ) CHAPTER 3. General Obligation and Construction of Contract [2301 - 2328] ( Chapter 3 enacted by Stats. 1963, Ch. 819. ) 2315. Where the seller at the time of contracting has reason to know any particular purpose for which the goods are required and that the buyer is relying on the seller’s skill or judgment to select or furnish suitable goods, there is unless excluded or modified under the next section an implied warranty that the goods shall be fit for such purpose. (Enacted by Stats. 1963, Ch. 819.)
perturbation.justification: The modified text contradicts California Commercial Code § 2315, specifically concerning implied warranties for fitness of purpose. The original text implies that risk of loss remains with the seller until delivery, whereas the changed text shifts the risk of loss to the buyer upon shipment. This contradicts the warranty as the buyer now assumes responsibility for the state of the goods at departure, potentially including pre-existing damages or non-conformities. As stated in Scraped Law Snippet 1, 'Where the seller at the time of contracting has reason to know any particular purpose for which the goods are required and that the buyer is relying on the seller’s skill or judgment to select or furnish suitable goods, there is unless excluded or modified under the next section an implied warranty that the goods shall be fit for such purpose.' The shift in risk nullifies the buyer's ability to hold the seller accountable for providing suitable goods.

perturbation.original_text: Each shipment of Product shall be accompanied by a certificate of analysis describing all current requirements of the Specifications and results of tests performed on such Product and a certificate of conformity certifying that the quantities of Product supplied have been Manufactured, controlled and released according to the Required Standards ("COA/COC") as set forth in the applicable Quality Agreement (subject to SutroVax's conduct of the SutroVax Activity Test to confirm Extract meets the SutroVax Activity Criteria). The COA/COC acceptance criteria for each Product shall be set forth in the Specification for such Product. Two of the tests and corresponding COA/COC acceptance criteria for the Extract shall be the performance of a productivity (Activity) test of the applicable Sutro protein (the "Sutro Activity Test" and "Sutro Activity Criteria") and the performance of a productivity (activity) test of the applicable Vaccine Composition (the "SutroVax Activity Test" and "SutroVax Activity Criteria"). Sutro shall perform the Sutro Activity Test to confirm that all shipments of Extract meet the Sutro Activity Criteria and SutroVax (or its designee) shall perform the SutroVax Activity Test to confirm that all shipments of Extract meet the SutroVax Activity Criteria. Sutro will also provide SutroVax with Material Safety Data Sheets ("MSDS") or an equivalent instrument recognized by the applicable Regulatory Authority as required for the Product(s), and updates of the same as necessary.
perturbation.changed_text: Each shipment of Product shall be accompanied by a certificate of analysis describing general testing results of such Product. Sutro will also provide SutroVax with Material Safety Data Sheets ("MSDS") or an equivalent instrument recognized by the applicable Regulatory Authority as necessary.
perturbation.explanation: The removed text guarantees that the product is Manufactured, controlled and released according to the required standards. The modified clause eliminates the requirement that Sutro certify the Product meets required standards. This weakens SutroVax's ability to verify the quality of the product upon receipt and its compliance with the law.
perturbation.law_explanation: 21 CFR § 211.165 requires that testing and release procedures include a review of production records to assure that errors have not occurred and that products meet specifications. By omitting the certification of conformity, the agreement contradicts the requirement to verify product standards. This is especially problematic for pharmaceutical products which require rigorous testing.

perturbation.justification: The original text requires a certificate of conformity that the product meets required standards and testing specifications, while the changed text omits this requirement. The Law Explanation states that 21 CFR § 211.165 requires review of production records to ensure products meet specifications. The scraped snippets do not provide the full text of 21 CFR 211.165, but the Law Explanation claims that omitting the certification contradicts the law by not verifying product standards. Given the removal of the certification requirement directly impacts the ability to verify standards, as claimed in the Law Explanation, this creates a clear and impactful contradiction under the Omission Legal Contradiction definition.

perturbation.original_text: Sutro shall replace any non-conforming Product(s) within the shortest possible time.
perturbation.changed_text: Sutro may replace any non-conforming Product(s) at its discretion.
perturbation.explanation: This modification changes Sutro's obligation to replace non-conforming goods into a discretionary action, removing a key consumer protection. This may violate California's implied warranty laws.
perturbation.law_explanation: California Commercial Code § 2714 grants buyers the right to damages for non-conformity of goods. By making the replacement of non-conforming goods discretionary, Sutro is effectively limiting the buyer's right to remedies for breach of warranty, thus contradicting consumer protection laws.
perturbation.scraped_snippet_1: California Code, COM 2714. skip to content home accessibility FAQ feedback sitemap login x Quick Search: Bill Number Bill Keyword Home Bill Information California Law Publications Other Resources My Subscriptions My Favorites California Law >> Code Search >> Code Section Code Section Code: Select Code CONS BPC CIV CCP COM CORP EDC ELEC EVID FAM FIN FGC FAC GOV HNC HSC INS LAB MVC PEN PROB PCC PRC PUC RTC SHC UIC VEH WAT WIC Article: Section: Code: Select Code All BPC CIV CCP COM CORP EDC ELEC EVID FAM FIN FGC FAC GOV HNC HSC INS LAB MVC PEN PROB PCC PRC PUC RTC SHC UIC VEH WAT WIC Section: Keyword(s): Code Search Text Search Up^ << Previous Next >> cross-reference chaptered bills PDF | Add To My Favorites Search Phrase: Code Text Commercial Code - COM DIVISION 2. SALES [2101 - 2801] ( Division 2 enacted by Stats. 1963, Ch. 819. ) CHAPTER 7. Remedies [2701 - 2725] ( Chapter 7 enacted by Stats. 1963, Ch. 819. ) 2714. (1) Where the buyer has accepted goods and given notification (subdivision (3) of Section 2607) he or she may recover, as damages for any nonconformity of tender, the loss resulting in the ordinary course of events from the seller’s breach as determined in any manner that is reasonable. (2) The measure of damages for breach of warranty is the difference at the time and place of acceptance between the value of the goods accepted and the value they would have had if they had been as warranted, unless special circumstances show proximate damages of a different amount. (3) In a proper case any incidental and consequential damages under Section 2715 also may be recovered. (Amended by Stats. 1995, Ch. 91, Sec. 21. Effective January 1, 1996.)
perturbation.justification: The changed text replaces the word 'shall' with 'may,' transforming a mandatory obligation for Sutro to replace non-conforming products into a discretionary one. This directly contradicts the principle outlined in California Commercial Code § 2714, as referenced in the Law Explanation, which grants buyers the right to damages for non-conformity of goods. By making the replacement discretionary, Sutro is effectively limiting the buyer's right to remedies and protections, which aligns with the Omission Legal Contradiction definition. Scraped Law Snippet 1, COM 2714 (1), states that a buyer 'may recover, as damages for any nonconformity of tender,' implying the buyer has a right to a remedy, which is undermined by Sutro's discretion.
